name: Berlin
blurb: A gritty, unvarnished look at Germany's capital from Cold War scars to world-class techno and cheap street eats.
imageAlt: The iconic Brandenburg Gate illuminated at dusk in central Berlin

name: Bremen
blurb: Forget Berlin's grit or Munich's polish. Bremen pairs medieval fairy-tale quarters with a rowdy, maritime grit and an obsession with local beer.
imageAlt: The historic Marktplatz in Bremen featuring the Gothic Town Hall and the famous Roland Statue under clear skies.

name: Hamburg
blurb: Germany's fierce, water-obsessed northern hub where grit meets high culture along legendary canals and harbor docks.
imageAlt: The historic illuminated Speicherstadt warehouse district reflecting in the canal waters at dusk

name: Lüneburg
blurb: A gorgeous medieval salt-mining town of brick Gothic facades, crooked half-timbered lanes, and water-side beer gardens that escaped WWII entirely unscathed.
imageAlt: The historic Alter Kran crane standing over the Ilmenau river at the Stintmarkt waterfront in Lüneburg

name: Lübeck
blurb: A striking medieval island city wrapped by the Trave River, famous as the historic capital of the Hanseatic League.
imageAlt: The iconic twin-towered medieval Holstentor gate in Lübeck under a clear sky

name: Munich
blurb: Bavaria's grand capital pairs stately neoclassical architecture and world-class museums with the unhurried, beer-garden-drenched ease of Gemütlichkeit.
imageAlt: The scenic view of the New Town Hall (Neues Rathaus) at Marienplatz in Munich city center.

name: Sachsenhausen Memorial
blurb: A haunting, deeply educational encounter with World War II and Cold War history at one of the earliest Nazi concentration camps.
imageAlt: The iron gates of Sachsenhausen Memorial featuring the infamous inscription Arbeit Macht Frei

name: Bremerhaven
blurb: Germany's rugged North Sea gateway ditches fairytale aesthetics for industrial weight, world-class modern museums, and some of the best raw seafood rolls you will ever eat.
imageAlt: Bremerhaven skyline showing the modern glass architecture of the Havenwelten district against the Weser River dike.

name: Potsdam
blurb: A grand playground of Prussian kings, spectacular royal estates, and vast formal gardens located just a short train ride from the capital.
imageAlt: The beautiful terraced vineyards leading up to Sanssouci Palace in Potsdam

name: Salzburg
blurb: A Baroque masterpiece framed by alpine peaks, Salzburg effortlessly blends classical musical heritage with a vibrant, historic beer culture.
imageAlt: Panoromic view of Salzburg historic old town with the fortress sitting on the hilltop

name: Schwerin
blurb: A stunning lakeside capital dominated by a fairytale island palace, offering rich history and a beautifully quiet northern German escape.
imageAlt: The golden spires of Schwerin Palace reflecting across the calm waters of the lake at sunset.

name: Bad Zwischenahn
blurb: Escape the city for a peaceful day at the Ammerland region's premier lake resort, packed with lush gardens, smoked delicacies, and classic thatched architecture.
imageAlt: The historic windmill sitting on the edge of the tranquil Zwischenahner Meer lake.

name: Oldenburg
blurb: A handsomely understated ducal city submerged in greenery, where bikes outnumber cars and North German hospitality rules the historic cobblestones.
imageAlt: A grand yellow building with historic Neoclassical and Renaissance architecture under a soft sky

name: Cuxhaven
blurb: Escape to where the Elbe meets the North Sea for windy beaches, historical lighthouses, and a unique walk across the ocean floor.
imageAlt: The Kugelbake wooden beacon standing on the shoreline of Cuxhaven under a dramatic North Sea sky

name: Dachau Memorial Site
blurb: A deeply moving, heavy, yet essential look at Germany's first concentration camp, located just outside Munich.
imageAlt: The infamous Jourhaus entrance gate at Dachau with the inscription Arbeit macht frei.

name: Travemünde
blurb: Trade the brick Gothic spires of Lübeck for the breezy Baltic coast, historic sailing ships, and the ultimate seaside street food.
imageAlt: Traditional wicker beach chairs lined up along the sandy Travemünde shore.

name: Hallstatt
blurb: Discover the fairytale alpine village of Hallstatt. Learn how to beat the daytime crowds, find the best lake views, and explore the world's oldest salt mine.
imageAlt: The iconic postcard view of Hallstatt showing the Evangelical church steeple against the calm waters of Hallstätter See and dramatic alpine peaks.

name: Neuschwanstein
blurb: Escape the city to see King Ludwig II's fairytale masterpiece nestled in the Bavarian Alps.
imageAlt: The white towers of Neuschwanstein Castle rising above dense green pine forests against an alpine backdrop.

name: Worpswede
blurb: Escape into the peaceful moorlands and explore the historic red-brick expressionist architecture and studios of Germany's most famous artists' colony.
imageAlt: The red-brick expressionist facade of the Grosse Kunstschau museum in Worpswede surrounded by green trees.

name: Bad Ischl
blurb: Trade the Alpine crowds of Hallstatt for the grand imperial spa town of Bad Ischl, famous for its thermal waters, royal palace, and world-class pastries.
imageAlt: The yellow facade of the historic imperial Kaiservilla surrounded by manicured green gardens in Bad Ischl.

name: Gosausee Alpine Lakes
blurb: A dramatic trio of glacial lakes framed by the jagged peaks of the Dachstein Alps, offering the perfect outdoor escape just outside Hallstatt.
imageAlt: The crystal clear waters of Vorderer Gosausee reflecting the snow-dusted Dachstein Glacier under a blue sky

name: Dachstein Krippenstein
blurb: Escape the crowded lakeside streets of Hallstatt for the jaw-dropping dramatic heights of the Dachstein Alps, complete with subterranean ice worlds and a thrilling walk over a 400-meter drop.
imageAlt: The Five Fingers viewing platform hanging over the sheer cliff face of Dachstein Krippenstein

name: Berchtesgaden & Eagle's Nest
blurb: Scale the Bavarian Alps to explore Adolf Hitler's former mountain retreat, stand among jagged alpine peaks, and uncover the dark history of the Obersalzberg complex.
imageAlt: The panoramic alpine mountain landscape looking out from the Kehlsteinhaus summit.

name: Untersberg Mountain
blurb: Escape the city for dramatic alpine views, high-altitude hiking trails, and a legendary mountain peak straddling the Austrian-German border.
imageAlt: The dramatic rocky ridge of Untersberg Mountain looking out over the surrounding alpine valley
